title: ASIC warns of crypto scams flooding social media groups. Scammers join WhatsApp stock tip chats and lure victims onto fake trading platforms with fabricated profits.
ATO and ASIC issue warnings about SMSF early access scams. Fraudsters target Australians via social media ads encouraging illegal superannuation withdrawals with high fees.
AFP alerts to business email compromise scams using gold bullion purchases. Three charged over $600,000 fraud where stolen funds bought gold to launder money.
Victorian man jailed for phone porting scam. 35-year-old sentenced to two years for stealing mobile numbers via identity theft to access banking accounts.
ASIC removes record 11,964 scam websites in 2025. AI-powered investment scams surge with 32 fake sites taken down daily, 90% increase from previous year.
ASIC cracks down on unlicensed finfluencers during global action week. Warning notices issued to four suspected of providing unlicensed financial advice online.
AFP warns of impersonation scams with fake bank calls. Scammers use hold music replication and tag-team tactics; one victim lost $350,000 cryptocurrency in 18 hours.
ACCC reports $2.18 billion lost to scams in 2025. Investment scams top list at $837.7 million, followed by payment redirection and romance scams with losses up 7.8%.
Scamwatch issues mobile fraud alert with ACMA. Criminals taking control of phone numbers via SIM-swapping and data breaches to access banking and crypto accounts.
AFP alerts to fake police crypto scam using ReportCyber. Criminals create false cybercrime reports then impersonate AFP officers to steal cryptocurrency seed phrases.
Scamwatch warns of Qantas impersonation scams via email and SMS. Fake messages claim refunds or expiring points directing victims to fraudulent websites.
ASIC urges superannuation funds to improve anti-scam protections. Review finds super funds lag far behind banks with only 40-60% meeting basic fraud prevention criteria.
